NSAIDs inhibit cyclo-oxygenase enzymes and decrease prostaglandin synthesis, thereby reducing pain, fever and inflammation.  They are used to treat many conditions such as headache, colds, flu, sore throat, pain, sprain, arthritis, osteoporosis, gout, menstrual cramps, and others.  NSAIDs can be taken orally or applied topically.  NSAIDs can cause side effects such as gastrointestinal problems, renal damage, and increased risk of bleeding.  In some cases, NSAIDs can cause serious complications such as myocardial infarction, stroke, and gastric ulcer.
